realization_fidelity.flattened_to: A decorative flower-animation visualization with direct point dragging that exposes the causal chain visually, but the code is incomplete, the trace strategy (ghost tangent lines) is missing, and the goal structure (finding three x-values) is not implemented—reducing it to pure free exploration without guidance or reflection mechanic.
violations: trace_strategy not implemented: no 'ghost' tangent lines or persistent visual history of previous positions; only current tangent line shown
goal_structure incomplete: the 'find three different x-values' prompt and any scaffolded discovery sequence are absent; code ends mid-implementation (truncated)

aha_moment delivery unclear: numerical displays of slope and f(x) values are written to state but no visual comparison widget or side-by-side numerical feedback is shown in the SVG to make the equality a learner discovery rather than a given
flower planting mechanic is extraneous decoration unrelated to the FTC design intent; diverts attention from core causal chain comparison
tangent line slope visualization present but no explicit numerical label or matching visual pairing with the f(x) height value on screen simultaneously

polish.worst_offense: The speedometer icon in the draggable handle at t≈4s reads as decorative emoji/clip-art inside the drawing surface, violating the no-decoration rule.
fixes: Remove the speedometer icon from the teal circular handle; replace with a plain accent-colored circle (12–14px radius) with soft shadow to signal affordance.
Ensure the purple (b) handle at t=8s has matching visual affordance: same radius, shadow, and hover scale-up; verify both are ≥24px hit area.
Confirm label positions ("Est: 0.0 | True: ???" and axis labels) remain unoccluded at all slider extremes; test with the handles dragged to their limits.

visual_check_failures.expected: Two stacked graphs visible: f(x) on top showing speed curve, A(x) below showing accumulated area. A vertical line marks the current x position. The shaded region shows accumulated area. Tangent line is visible on A(x).
visual_check_failures.observed: The interface appears virtually identical between the two screenshots with no visible changes to the graph layout, additional stacked graphs, shaded regions, or tangent lines.

visual_check_failures.expected: Vertical line moves to x=4, shaded region expands, A(x) value increases, tangent line on A(x) updates
visual_check_failures.observed: The green circle indicator moved slightly along the curve but the vertical line remained at the same position and the shaded region did not expand visibly.

visual_check_failures.expected: The slope readout on the tangent line numerically matches the f(x) height readout above, demonstrating A'(x) = f(x)
visual_check_failures.observed: The two screenshots appear nearly identical with no visible changes to slope readouts, tangent lines, or numerical displays.

visual_check_failures.expected: Shaded region under f(x) expands as x moves right, A(x) readout increases
visual_check_failures.observed: The two screenshots appear identical with no visible changes to shading, readout values, or any other visual elements.

visual_check_failures.expected: Left bound of shaded region stays fixed at a=0, only right bound moves with x
visual_check_failures.observed: The shaded region appears to have moved slightly to the right, with both bounds shifting rather than only the right bound moving while the left bound stays fixed at a=0.

results.description: On load, the visualization shows the two graphs but the relationship is not yet obvious - inviting exploration
results.visual_check: Two stacked graphs visible: f(x) on top showing speed curve, A(x) below showing accumulated area. A vertical line marks the current x position. The shaded region shows accumulated area. Tangent line is visible on A(x).

visual_verdict.observed: The interface appears virtually identical between the two screenshots with no visible changes to the graph layout, additional stacked graphs, shaded regions, or tangent lines.

results.description: The x-position can be dragged horizontally along the axis
results.visual_check: Vertical line moves to x=4, shaded region expands, A(x) value increases, tangent line on A(x) updates

visual_verdict.observed: The green circle indicator moved slightly along the curve but the vertical line remained at the same position and the shaded region did not expand visibly.

results.description: The slope of A(x) at position x approximately equals f(x) at the same position - the core FTC insight
results.visual_check: The slope readout on the tangent line numerically matches the f(x) height readout above, demonstrating A'(x) = f(x)

visual_verdict.observed: The two screenshots appear nearly identical with no visible changes to slope readouts, tangent lines, or numerical displays.

results.description: Moving x changes the accumulated area A(x)
results.visual_check: Shaded region under f(x) expands as x moves right, A(x) readout increases

visual_verdict.observed: The two screenshots appear identical with no visible changes to shading, readout values, or any other visual elements.

results.description: The left bound a is frozen from previous section and cannot be changed
results.visual_check: Left bound of shaded region stays fixed at a=0, only right bound moves with x

visual_verdict.observed: The shaded region appears to have moved slightly to the right, with both bounds shifting rather than only the right bound moving while the left bound stays fixed at a=0.
